forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
+- 01KXTFV39FC09ZS0CZ1X3NGGMX
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8a80
+title: 'Evals: FoundationModelsRouterEvals target + CompactionEvaluation'
+---
+## What
+Compaction quality evals on Apple's Evaluations framework (WWDC26), per compaction_plan.md ┬¦5: a new gated test target `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterEvals/` (add to `Package.swift`, gated on `FM_ROUTER_INTEGRATION_TESTS` like other real-model tests).
+
+`CompactionEvaluation: Evaluation`:
+- **Subject**: compact the sample's transcript with the prompt under test, resume a session over the result, ask the sample's question ŌĆö return answer + `CompactionResult`.
+- **Dataset**: 20ŌĆō30 hand-written seed transcripts (`ArrayLoader<PlantedFacts>`) with varied lengths, tool traffic, and multiple planted facts in the transcript head ("the API key lives in `.env.example`", "we chose tabs over spaces"); questions answerable only from folded content. Scale later with SampleGenerator.
+- **Evaluators**: mechanical `FactRetention` metric (answer contains the planted fact), mechanical `UnderTarget` metric (`tokensAfter <= targetTokens`), and a `ModelJudgeEvaluator` (judge Ōēź as capable as subject) scoring Faithfulness and Continuability on a four-point scale.
+- Test entry: `@Test(.evaluates(...))` asserting mean FactRetention Ōēź 0.9 (see the sketch in compaction_plan.md ┬¦5).
+
+The same evaluation pointed at different `CompactionPrompt`s (the segment records the prompt name) is the hill-climbing loop for the default prompt ŌĆö structure the dataset/runner so a prompt name is a parameter.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Evals target builds; excluded from the default `swift test` run when ungated
+- [ ] Dataset of Ōēź 20 seed transcripts with planted facts committed as fixtures
+- [ ] FactRetention, UnderTarget, and the two judged dimensions all report; the gated test asserts mean FactRetention Ōēź 0.9
+- [ ] Running the evaluation with two different prompt names yields per-prompt attributable results
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterEvals/CompactionEvaluationTests.swift` ŌĆö the gated `@Test(.evaluates(...))`; passes with `FM_ROUTER_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1`
+- [ ] A hermetic smoke test that the dataset loads and the subject closure wires up against a fake model (no real inference), in the same target or `FoundationModelsRouterTests`; passes in plain `swift test`
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,22 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky94aef21mt5zze1v8zdnr9s
+  text: |-
+    Implemented. Key research finding before writing any code: "Apple's Evaluations framework (WWDC26)" referenced in compaction_plan.md ┬¦5 is a REAL framework, not aspirational ŌĆö but it does not live in the regular SDK frameworks path (grepping MacOSX27.0.sdk for "Evaluations" turns up nothing). It ships as a *developer-only* framework analogous to XCTest: `/Applications/Xcode-beta.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/Library/Frameworks/Evaluations.framework`. Confirmed empirically that SwiftPM's `.testTarget` automatically adds the toolchain's test-framework search path (the same one that makes `import Testing`/XCTest work) ŌĆö a throwaway SwiftPM package with a bare `import Evaluations` in a `.testTarget` builds and runs with zero unsafe flags/linker settings. So `Package.swift` needed nothing beyond an ordinary `.testTarget` declaration.
+
+    Read the actual installed `Evaluations.framework` `.swiftinterface` (not guessed from the plan's pseudocode) since the plan's own sketch doesn't match the real API shape: `Evaluation.Sample.ExpectedValue` must equal `Subject.Value` (an associated-type equality constraint), so `CompactionEvaluationOutcome` is one Codable struct playing double duty ŌĆö ground truth when it's a sample's `expected`, produced results when it's a subject's `value`. `ModelJudgeEvaluator<Input>` requires `Input: ModelSampleProtocol`, which forced using Apple's own `ModelSample<ExpectedValue>` rather than a fully custom `SampleProtocol` conformer.
+
+    Files added under `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterEvals/`: `CompactionEvalDataset.swift` (24 hand-written fixture specs ŌĆö single/multi-fact heads, tool-traffic variants, varied lengths), `CompactionEvalSeed.swift` (assembles fixtures into real `Transcript.Entry` sequences, including simulated tool call/output pairs), `CompactionEvaluationOutcome.swift`, `CompactionEvaluation.swift` (the `Evaluation` conformer ŌĆö `prompt`/`budget` are init parameters, so pointing the same type at two different `CompactionPrompt`s is the hill-climbing loop), `CompactionEvaluationTests.swift` (hermetic dataset/subject/prompt-attribution tests + the gated `@Test(.evaluates(...))` asserting mean FactRetention >= 0.9), `Support/CompactionEvalRealSubjectRunner.swift` (lazy-loading actor wrapping a real MLX model for the gated path ŌĆö kept lazy so `CompactionEvaluation`/`.evaluates(...)` can be constructed synchronously, which the trait's attribute-position argument requires).
+
+    Budget default (`TokenBudget(limit: 4000, target: 0.05)`) is deliberately aggressive: forces the pipeline past `ToolOutputElision`/`TurnTruncation` into the model-assisted `Summarization` stage every time, since `TurnTruncation` alone would drop the planted fact with zero trace (no summary entry) if it happened to land under a looser target first.
+
+    Gated real-model test cannot execute in this sandbox (no network/GPU, consistent with every other `FM_ROUTER_INTEGRATION_TESTS`-gated suite already in this repo) ŌĆö compiles and is correctly skipped via `.enabled(if:)` when the env var is unset.
+
+    Verified: clean `swift test` ŌĆö 482 tests in `FoundationModelsRouterTests` (matches stated baseline, no regression), 17 in `FoundationModelsRouterIntegrationTests` (all appropriately gated/skipped), 4 new hermetic tests in `FoundationModelsRouterEvals` (all pass), gated eval test correctly skipped. Zero warnings beyond one pre-existing, unrelated build-system warning about an mlx-swift bundle mutation.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T03:57:18.178560+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
 - 01KXTFV39FC09ZS0CZ1X3NGGMX

\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #compaction
+
+## Implementation Notes (2026-07-24)
+
+"Apple's Evaluations framework (WWDC26)" is a real, installed framework in this sandbox's Xcode-beta toolchain ŌĆö it lives at `Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/Library/Frameworks/Evaluations.framework` (a developer-only framework analogous to XCTest, not in the regular SDK frameworks path). SwiftPM's `.testTarget` automatically adds the required test-framework search path, so `Package.swift` needed only an ordinary `.testTarget` declaration ŌĆö no unsafe flags.
+
+The framework's real API (read from its `.swiftinterface`, not guessed from the plan's pseudocode) requires `Evaluation.Sample.ExpectedValue == Subject.Value`, so `CompactionEvaluationOutcome` is one `Codable` struct serving as both a sample's ground truth and a subject's produced result. `ModelJudgeEvaluator` requires `ModelSampleProtocol`, so the dataset uses Apple's own `ModelSample<CompactionEvaluationOutcome>` rather than a fully custom sample type.
+
+24 hand-written fixtures (`CompactionEvalDataset.swift`/`CompactionEvalSeed.swift`) cover single- and multi-fact heads, tool-traffic variants, and varied lengths. `CompactionEvaluation.swift` implements the `Evaluation` conformer with `prompt`/`budget` as init parameters (the hill-climbing loop is literally constructing a second `CompactionEvaluation` value). The gated real-model path (`Support/CompactionEvalRealSubjectRunner.swift`) is a lazy-loading actor so the evaluation and its `.evaluates(...)` trait argument can be constructed synchronously while the real MLX model load is deferred to first actual use.
+
+Verified via clean `swift test`: 482 tests in `FoundationModelsRouterTests` (matches stated baseline, no regression), 17 in `FoundationModelsRouterIntegrationTests` (all gated/skipped as before), 4 new hermetic tests in `FoundationModelsRouterEvals` (all pass), the one gated eval test correctly skipped without `FM_ROUTER_INTEGRATION_TESTS` set. Cannot execute the gated real-model test in this sandbox (no network/GPU) ŌĆö same documented limitation as every other gated suite in this repo.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -17,6 +17,19 @@

     Verified: clean `swift test` ŌĆö 482 tests in `FoundationModelsRouterTests` (matches stated baseline, no regression), 17 in `FoundationModelsRouterIntegrationTests` (all appropriately gated/skipped), 4 new hermetic tests in `FoundationModelsRouterEvals` (all pass), gated eval test correctly skipped. Zero warnings beyond one pre-existing, unrelated build-system warning about an mlx-swift bundle mutation.
   timestamp: 2026-07-24T03:57:18.178560+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky9503h5g42rszq8batdjkmb
+  text: |-
+    Adversarial double-check (via really-done) found 3 issues on the first implementation pass. Fixed 2, logging a justification for the 3rd:
+
+    1. FIXED (critical) ŌĆö `FactRetention` evaluator checked `answer.contains(plantedFact)` where `plantedFact` was the full declarative sentence (e.g. "The project's internal vault code is CRIMSON-77; it must be remembered precisely."). A short, targeted answer ("CRIMSON-77") can never contain that whole sentence as a substring, so the metric would fail unconditionally regardless of whether compaction preserved the fact ŌĆö this exact backwards direction was inherited verbatim from compaction_plan.md ┬¦5's own pseudocode sketch. Fix: added a `factKeyPhrase` field to every fixture (`CompactionEvalFixtureSpec`/`CompactionEvalSeed`/`CompactionEvaluationOutcome`) carrying the short, distinctive token/value a correct answer should contain (e.g. "CRIMSON-77", "6543", "AES-256-GCM"), threaded through the dataset/subject path, and changed the evaluator to check `answer.contains(factKeyPhrase)` instead of the full sentence.
+
+    2. FIXED (coverage gap) ŌĆö no hermetic test verified the default `TokenBudget(limit: 4000, target: 0.05)` actually forces the pipeline past `ToolOutputElision`/`TurnTruncation` into the model-assisted `Summarization` stage (the only stage that leaves a checkable trace of the fact). Added `defaultBudgetForcesSummarizationStage` to `CompactionEvaluationTests.swift`: runs the real `Compactor.compact` directly against all 24 `compactionEvalSeeds` with a trivial fake summarizer and asserts every one reaches `stagesApplied == ["ToolOutputElision", "TurnTruncation", "Summarization"]`. Passes.
+
+    3. JUSTIFICATION LOGGED, not fixed ŌĆö the `ModelJudgeEvaluator` in `CompactionEvaluation.evaluators` omits an explicit `judge:` parameter, defaulting to the framework's own `SystemLanguageModel()` rather than a model "Ōēź as capable as subject" (compaction_plan.md ┬¦5's stated preference, since the gated eval's subject model is a 27B-parameter dense model, likely more capable than Apple's on-device default). Not fixed because doing so correctly would require a custom `FoundationModels.LanguageModel` conformer that defers its real model load to first actual use ŌĆö `LanguageModel` is a large, multi-associated-type protocol, and Swift Testing's `.evaluates(...)` trait argument is evaluated synchronously at test-registration time, so a real MLX-backed judge can't be constructed directly the way `runSubject`'s closure-based DI sidesteps this for the subject path. Given (a) this gated test cannot execute in this sandbox anyway so the choice can't be empirically verified either way, (b) it is a quality/capability nuance rather than a correctness bug affecting the mechanical FactRetention/UnderTarget metrics or the acceptance criteria ("the two judged dimensions all report" ŌĆö satisfied regardless of which judge model is wired in), and (c) `SystemLanguageModel()` is the framework's own documented default for exactly this parameter, proceeding without further engineering here. Left as a documented trade-off in the code comment above `ModelJudgeEvaluator<Sample>(...)` in CompactionEvaluation.swift.
+
+    Re-verified after fixes: `swift test` clean ŌĆö 482 + 17 + 5 = 504 tests, all green, zero new warnings.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T04:09:07.877601+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
 - 01KXTFV39FC09ZS0CZ1X3NGGMX

-    1. FIXED (critical) ŌĆö `FactRetention` evaluator checked `answer.contains(plantedFact)` where `plantedFact` was the full declarative sentence (e.g. "The project's internal vault code is CRIMSON-77; it must be remembered precisely."). A short, targeted answer ("CRIMSON-77") can never contain that whole sentence as a substring, so the metric would fail unconditionally regardless of whether compaction preserved the fact ŌĆö this exact backwards direction was inherited verbatim from compaction_plan.md ┬¦5's own pseudocode sketch. Fix: added a `factKeyPhrase` field to every fixture (`CompactionEvalFixtureSpec`/`CompactionEvalSeed`/`CompactionEvaluationOutcome`) carrying the short, distinctive token/value a correct answer should contain (e.g. "CRIMSON-77", "6543", "AES-256-GCM"), threaded through the dataset/subject path, and changed the evaluator to check `answer.contains(factKeyPhrase)` instead of the full sentence.
-
-    2. FIXED (coverage gap) ŌĆö no hermetic test verified the default `TokenBudget(limit: 4000, target: 0.05)` actually forces the pipeline past `ToolOutputElision`/`TurnTruncation` into the model-assisted `Summarization` stage (the only stage that leaves a checkable trace of the fact). Added `defaultBudgetForcesSummarizationStage` to `CompactionEvaluationTests.swift`: runs the real `Compactor.compact` directly against all 24 `compactionEvalSeeds` with a trivial fake summarizer and asserts every one reaches `stagesApplied == ["ToolOutputElision", "TurnTruncation", "Summarization"]`. Passes.
-
-    3. JUSTIFICATION LOGGED, not fixed ŌĆö the `ModelJudgeEvaluator` in `CompactionEvaluation.evaluators` omits an explicit `judge:` parameter, defaulting to the framework's own `SystemLanguageModel()` rather than a model "Ōēź as capable as subject" (compaction_plan.md ┬¦5's stated preference, since the gated eval's subject model is a 27B-parameter dense model, likely more capable than Apple's on-device default). Not fixed because doing so correctly would require a custom `FoundationModels.LanguageModel` conformer that defers its real model load to first actual use ŌĆö `LanguageModel` is a large, multi-associated-type protocol, and Swift Testing's `.evaluates(...)` trait argument is evaluated synchronously at test-registration time, so a real MLX-backed judge can't be constructed directly the way `runSubject`'s closure-based DI sidesteps this for the subject path. Given (a) this gated test cannot execute in this sandbox anyway so the choice can't be empirically verified either way, (b) it is a quality/capability nuance rather than a correctness bug affecting the mechanical FactRetention/UnderTarget metrics or the acceptance criteria ("the two judged dimensions all report" ŌĆö satisfied regardless of which judge model is wired in), and (c) `SystemLanguageModel()` is the framework's own documented default for exactly this parameter, proceeding without further engineering here. Left as a documented trade-off in the code comment above `ModelJudgeEvaluator<Sample>(...)` in CompactionEvaluation.swift.
-
